commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lexify
repository.description: Headless, plugin-driven Rich Text Editor built on Lexical

Co-authored-by: wzshiming <6565744+wzshiming@users.noreply.github.com>

v1.4.4: simplify image capture to v1.3.4 Qt-only path

Remove over-engineered Win32 image fallback machinery:
  - Remove _try_capture_image, _retry_image_capture, _retry_pending
  - Remove _read_clipboard_format_bytes, _open_clipboard_with_retry
  - Remove _CF_PNG / _CF_JFIF module-level registration
  - Remove CF_BITMAP, CF_DIB, CF_DIBV5 constants
  - Remove kernel32 / ctypes restype fixups (no longer needed)
  - Remove 'import time'

Replace with the proven v1.3.4 inline approach in _process_clipboard:
  if mime.hasImage():
      img = clipboard.image()
  Uses bytesPerLine()*height() for safe QImage hash (not sizeInBytes).

All other new features retained: threading/_lock, suppress_next(),
set_exclusions(), _is_excluded_app(), _clipboard_has_exclude_flag()
(via IsClipboardFormatAvailable only), WM_WTSSESSION_CHANGE,
on_session_unlocked(), TYPE_EMAIL, TYPE_CODE, _looks_like_code(),
detect_sensitive(), _image_hash().

All 8 integration tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial release: APK Reverse Engineering Platform v0.1.0

Automated API discovery from Android apps combining static analysis
(jadx, string scanning, Dart binary extraction) with dynamic capture
(Frida SSL hooks, logcat Dio parsing, mitmproxy) to produce OpenAPI
3.0 specs, Postman collections, and curl command sheets.

Key features:
- Interactive capture mode with real-time endpoint discovery
- Auto-pull APK from device (no local file needed)
- Auto-detect ADB device
- Telemetry/analytics noise filtering
- Split APK handling for App Bundles
- JWT token extraction from heap dumps and SharedPreferences
- BoringSSL pattern scanning for Flutter release builds

Includes Bambu Handy analysis examples (50+ endpoints across 14
microservices discovered from live capture).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: apkre
repository.description: APK Reverse Engineering Platform — automated API discovery from Android apps. Extracts endpoints, auth tokens, schemas, and generates OpenAPI 3.0 specs via static analysis + dynamic capture (Frida, logcat, mitmproxy).

commit.message:
feat: Tool Loadouts / Toolbelts for Agent Tooling (#116)

* Initial plan

* Initial plan for Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature with tests

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add comprehensive Tool Loadout documentation

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add tool discovery system with auto-loadout scoping

Adds a dynamic tool discovery mechanism that allows agents to start with
a minimal toolset and discover/activate additional tools on demand. This
prevents context window overflow on small-context models (e.g. Foundry
Local 28K) while keeping all tools accessible.

New features:
- ToolDiscoveryTools: 4 kernel functions (discover_tools, activate_tool,
  list_tool_categories, list_toolbelts) for runtime tool management
- Auto-loadout selection: maps context window size to appropriate loadout
  (>=128K -> full, >=64K -> developer, <64K -> minimal)
- Loadout-aware scoping replaces crude "disable all tools" approach
- toolDiscovery plugin always preserved across all loadouts

Also fixes flaky SlidingWindowRateLimiter test with retry loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve formatting issues in SubagentRunner and ToolLoadoutTests

Remove alignment padding in switch expressions and fix import ordering
to satisfy dotnet format CI checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Jerrett D. Davis <mxjerrett@gmail.com>

commit.message:
perf(eval): skip test discovery on expression-only evals

Test discovery via LiveTestingHook.afterReload now only runs when:
- Methods were actually updated (function/type definitions), OR
- First eval needs initial project assembly scan

Expression-only evals (e.g. '1+1;;') skip discovery entirely,
eliminating the synchronous reflection overhead that caused slow
repeated evals.

Fixes #11

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- virtual-machine-scale-sets.md - Auto-scaling VM groups
- azure-virtual-desktop.md - Desktop virtualization (AVD)
- azure-managed-disks.md - Managed disk lifecycle and encryption
- azure-cache-for-redis.md - In-memory caching layer

commit.message:
simplify starting the dev server from scratch (#5433)

* feat: add prestart/prestartSSL hooks to auto-build prerequisites

Automatically runs buildDLL:dev and buildModules before starting the
dev server if common-dist/common-manifest.json or package build outputs
are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move prestart build check into shared script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check every buildable package for dist/index.js in prestart script

Previously used a glob that only checked if any dist/index.js existed,
not all of them. Now iterates packages with a build script to ensure
every one has its artifact before skipping buildModules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use next available port if 3000 is already in use

Uses webpack-dev-server's built-in 'auto' port selection so yarn start
doesn't fail with EADDRINUSE when port 3000 is taken. Can still be
overridden with PORT=<n> yarn start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert: remove auto port selection change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add set -euo pipefail to prestart script for fail-fast behavior

Ensures a failed buildDLL:dev or buildModules stops the process
immediately rather than continuing and hitting the same missing
artifact error downstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update pos-customer/openapi.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests (#155)

* feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests

Add processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating) to
replace binary _isProcessing flag. Show animated "thinking..." indicator
during request setup and "generating..." during streaming. Add stop
button that cancels in-flight requests via session.abort().

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add processing phase and stop button tests

Add 7 tests covering the processing phase state machine:
- thinking/generating/idle phase transitions
- idle-on-error recovery
- full lifecycle sequence
- stopRequest triggering session.abort()
- stopRequest no-op when idle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* squad: progress indicator + stop button orchestration logs, session summary, and decision merge

Scribe merge:
- Orchestration logs for MacReady (architecture), Blair (implementation), Windows (testing)
- Session log for progress indicator + stop button work
- Merged 3 decision inbox files into decisions.md (no duplicates; consolidated into single entry)
- Deleted inbox files after merge

Issue resolved: #122 (Stop Button Robustness)

Work summary:
- 4-state processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating → idle)
- Progress indicator (animated dots) with dynamic text
- Stop button with session.abort() cancellation
- 7 new tests, all 301 tests passing
- Zero regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: swap context bar layout and merge send/stop button

Move attach buttons (Selection, File) to left side with workspace
indicator on the right. Replace separate stop button with send/stop
toggle — Send button becomes Stop during processing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — stop/cancel lifecycle and race conditions

- Add request-scoped cancellation flag for thinking phase stop
- Track request ID to prevent finally-block clobbering
- Properly await session.abort() with error logging
- Add safety timeout for stuck stop button state
- Block duplicate sends during thinking phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add cancel lifecycle and race condition tests

Tests for thinking-phase cancellation, request ID clobbering
prevention, abort error handling, and duplicate send prevention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update windows history with cancel lifecycle testing learnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

feat: add invisible pane borders after colorscheme load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(daemon): filter resumed sessions by working directory

Only resume sessions whose WorkingDir is under the daemon's current
directory (or vice versa). Sessions from unrelated directories are
logged and skipped instead of loading unconditionally.

Closes #28, Closes #27

feat: Phase 18 — Offline Smart Mode with local LLM

Add local LLM support via llama-server subprocess (SmolLM2-360M):
- llm.go: Managed subprocess lifecycle (start/stop/health-check)
- llm_download.go: Download llama-server + GGUF model from releases
- Provider selection: OpenAI / Local / Auto with fallback chain
- Keyword-based template matching against active app (EXE + title)
- Default keywords for all 12 builtin presets (email, meeting, etc.)
- GetActiveWindowTitle() via Win32 GetWindowTextW
- Template metadata: description + keywords for auto-detection
- Configurable fallback preset (default: cleanup)
- Premium UI: provider segmented control, LLM download card,
  fallback preset dropdown, keyword chip input for custom templates
- Full EN + DE localization (28 new translation keys)

- Guard JSON.parse in rowToWebhook with try/catch, default to []
- Guard JSON.parse in rowToSavedSearch with try/catch, default to null
- Guard JSON.parse in loadDbConnectorConfig with try/catch, throw ConfigError
- Push dateFrom/dateTo filters into SQL WHERE in listDocuments (before LIMIT)

- Replace manual substring extension parsing with path.extname() in packs.ts
- Verified reporter.ts is already tracked on development (no action needed)

Closes #342

commit.message:
Add ambiguity prevention rules and expand word substitutions for grade 12→8 push (#8)

* Initial plan

* Add ambiguity prevention rules and expanded word substitutions to push grade 12→8

Based on real-world research (Arkansas AG Opinion 2025-046, NY S1381A):
- Add AMBIGUITY PREVENTION section (8 rules) to system prompt
- Add AMBIGUITY CHECK to self-check section
- Add anti-ambiguity rules to refinement and targeted refinement prompts
- Add 10 new phrase substitutions (e.g., 'for the purpose of' → 'to')
- Add 13 new word substitutions (e.g., 'expenditure' → 'spending')
- Add 20+ new word/phrase substitution guidance to system prompt
- Add 24 new tests covering all new functionality
- All 121 tests pass

* Address code review: fix inasmuch→inasmuch as, acquisition→purchase, distribution→sharing

---------

commit.message:
Complete Phase 2.3 + Phase 3: ADRs and UX design system

ADRs (4 files):
- 001-frontend-framework.md: Next.js 14 + shadcn/ui + Tailwind (Accepted)
- 002-backend-framework.md: NestJS with Fastify adapter (Accepted)
- 003-database.md: PostgreSQL 16 with closure table (Accepted)
- 004-auth-strategy.md: Keycloak 24 CAC/PKI + hierarchical RBAC (Accepted)

UX Design (4 files):
- design-system.md: USWDS-based palette, system fonts, tokens, dark mode
- wireframes.md: ASCII wireframes for all 6 core views

commit.message:
refactor: decompose Program.cs — extract CLI parser, provider orchestrator, session configurator (#119)

Extract 3 focused modules from the 1,437-line Program.cs god file:

- CliOptions + CliArgumentParser: strongly-typed CLI flag parsing (all 30+ flags)
- ProviderOrchestrator: provider detection, model listing, model selection
- SessionConfigurator: session creation, flag application, persistence/resume/fork/worktree

Program.cs reduced from 1,437 to 999 lines (-30%).
Remaining sections (tool registration, governance, skills, TUI loop) will be
extracted in subsequent PRs.

Build: 0 warnings, 0 errors
Tests: 2,150+ passed (1 pre-existing clipboard env failure)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add async SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM module with AUTOCOMMIT, parallel queries, and frozen results (#163)

* Initial plan

* feat: add async SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM module and tests

- Add src/common/database.py with AsyncSession patterns, AUTOCOMMIT
  readonly sessions, asyncio.gather() parallel queries, frozen results,
  DDL helpers, and a health-check ping utility
- Add tests/unit/common/test_database.py with 27 tests (26 pass, 1 skipped)
- Add aiosqlite>=0.19.0 to dev dependencies for in-process SQLite testing
- SQLAlchemy already at >=2.0.46 (>= target 2.0.44)

Co-authored-by: theinterneti <169108167+theinterneti@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate PropagandaSystem to SingleComponentSystem<PropagandaNetwork> template base

- Header: Replace ecs::System base with ecs::SingleComponentSystem<components::PropagandaNetwork>
- Header: Remove update() override, add protected updateComponent() override
- Source: Change constructor initializer from System(world) to SingleComponentSystem(world)
- Source: Replace update() entity-loop with updateComponent() operating on a single component reference
- Retain world_->getEntities<>() patterns in API methods that search across all entities

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: NovaForge
repository.description: NovaForge is an ambitious PVE‑focused space simulation project, inspired by large‑scale space universes like EVE Online but scaled down for smaller groups and cooperative play without PvP focus. Built in C++ with OpenGL on top of the custom Atlas Engine, NovaForge aims to deliver a dynamic living universe.

commit.message:
feat(mobile): Account detail page, creation form, and transaction history view

- AccountDetailScreen (task 13.2): Balance summary with utilisation bar, account info, recent transactions, action buttons
- AccountCreationForm (task 13.3): Validated form with payment terms pills, inline errors, keyboard-safe ScrollView
- TransactionHistoryView (task 13.4): Filterable FlatList with type filters, date range, date-grouped rows, summary footer
- 19 component tests covering render, interaction, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make idle→wander test deterministic by isolating herbivore

The test was flaky in CI because randomly-spawned carnivores near
the herbivore would cause flee transitions, disrupting the expected
idle/wander toggle count. Now uses a controlled, isolated creature.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
